data.content: Starting `dotnet watch test` for you — this will re-run tests automatically whenever you save changes.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to set up `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so it runs continuously and re-runs tests when files change.

Let me start it up.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-5307f4c41c114e21ade63ced518e7a0b && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for file changes. 

There's currently a build error — `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` line 25 is missing `using System;` for `ArgumentException`. Also, the express test expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00`.

The watcher will automatically re-run tests each time you save a file. Just edit and save — no need to manually re-run anything.
data.reasoningText:
The watch is running. There's a build error — missing `using System;` for `ArgumentException`. But the user said they have failing tests and want hot reload to iterate. I should tell them it's running and point out the current error, and let them fix it.

Actually, the watcher is running and waiting for changes. The current error is a missing `using System;`. Let me inform the user that the watcher is active, and note the current compilation error.
